analysis_metadata.title: Open-data proxy reinterpretation of same-charge leptons plus b-jets selections
analysis_metadata.reference_analysis_title: Search for new phenomena in events with same-charge leptons and b-jets in pp collisions at sqrt(s) = 13 TeV with the ATLAS detector
analysis_metadata.analysis_scope_note: This JSON intentionally describes what can be executed with the available ATLAS open-data ntuples. It preserves the same-charge dilepton/trilepton object and region structure, but does not request official VLQ mass limits, full CLs limits, fake-lepton matrix-method reproduction, or electron charge-misID calibration when the required signal grids and auxiliary inputs are absent.

analysis_objectives.target: same-charge dilepton and trilepton plus b-jets open-data proxy analysis
signal_models: available heavy-top or other top-rich proxy samples when present
SM four-top sample as a named comparison sample when present

explicit list of blocked official VLQ/CLs/mass-limit claims

signal_signatures.selection_specific_note: Only positively charged lepton pairs are used because tt production is much larger than tbar tbar production in pp collisions.

nuisance_parameter_classes.details: Control-sample definition, control-sample statistics, and prompt-subtraction normalization; dominant component typically around 40-50% of the fake/non-prompt yield in the signal regions.
About 20% of the expected charge-misID yield in the signal regions, plus an anti-correlated component from fake/non-prompt subtraction.

results.statement: This open-data target does not request official VLQ, four-top, or same-sign-top exclusions. It requests reproducible diagnostic yields and counting-proxy sensitivities for the available samples.

results.blocking_reason: The required dedicated signal grids, full likelihood/nuisance model, fake-lepton matrix method, and charge-misID calibration inputs are not available in the open-data workspace.

allowed_outputs: central sample registry and sample exclusion reasons
cutflows with IO prefilters separated from physics cuts
signal-region yields for data, central backgrounds, and named signal proxies
diagnostic expected sensitivity and observed count comparisons using a fixed simplified model

analysis_design_insights.statement: For the VLQ and four-top searches, the preselected sample is first split by lepton multiplicity (2l or 3l) and b-tag multiplicity (1, 2, or >=3), and then by HT and ETmiss requirements.

Signal regions with at least three b-tagged jets are further split: relaxed HT plus high jet multiplicity is used for four-top sensitivity, while high HT plus lower jet multiplicity enhances VLQ sensitivity.

VLQ/four-top regions use inclusive lepton flavours to increase the statistics of the loosely selected samples used for reducible-background estimation, whereas the same-sign top search splits ee, e mu, and mu mu because the looser optimized selection provides enough statistics.
The same-sign top search uses exactly two positive leptons, HT > 750 GeV, ETmiss > 40 GeV, at least one b-tagged jet, and |delta phi_ll| > 2.5.
Validation regions kinematically close to the signal regions are defined to test the background model; the smallest reported validation-region p-value is 0.10.

For the open-data proxy workflow, reducible fake/non-prompt and charge-misID backgrounds are not reproduced as official data-driven estimates; their regions are retained as diagnostics while central yields use available simulated groups with explicit caveats.
Statistical outputs are diagnostic counting proxies. Official CLs, mass limits, and coupling limits are blocked unless a dedicated signal grid and full nuisance/correlation model are added.

leptons.definition: entries in lep_* arrays with abs(lep_type) in {11,13}, nonzero charge, |eta| < 2.5, and sig_lep != 0 when available
leptons.quality_source: open-data sig_lep branch; tight/isolation/impact-parameter details are not re-derived unless sig_lep is unavailable

electrons.eta_requirement: open-data proxy uses |lep_eta| < 2.5 plus sig_lep; exact official electron crack-veto status is not separately resolvable from a dedicated branch

jets.overlap_removal: DeltaR(jet, selected lepton) > 0.4 computed from jet_phi/eta and lep_phi/eta

b_tagging.official_working_point_claim: blocked unless an external calibration mapping is added

trigger.policy: open-data trigger approximation; exact official multilepton trigger strategy is not reproduced

same_charge_lepton_category.definition: If exactly two of the three highest-pT leptons satisfy the nominal criteria, they must have the same electric charge.

trilepton_category.definition: If the three highest-pT leptons satisfy the nominal criteria, no requirement is imposed on charge or pair invariant mass.

validation_regions.lepton_charge_requirement: e- e- or e- mu- or mu- mu-

retrieval_features.validation_region_rule: Events that appear in any signal region are vetoed in the validation regions.
background_estimation_tags: MC-modeled irreducible backgrounds when matching samples exist
statistical_method_tags: expected model fixed before observed count comparison
no official CLs exclusion without full likelihood inputs
sensitivity_drivers: jet multiplicity split in >=3 b-tag regions
same-sign dilepton and trilepton final states
large azimuthal separation between leptons in same-sign top search
signal_target_tags: reference VLQ topology without mass-limit claim
retrieval_features.fit_observable: event counts per signal region or approved family diagnostic

lepton_flavour_strategy.same_sign_tt: split into ee, e mu, and mu mu

central_signal_policy.primary_signal_proxy: available BSM four-top-like or heavy-top proxy MC, selected by the pipeline from local samples

background_policy.irreducible_backgrounds: simulation-driven from available ttW, ttZ, ttH, diboson, four-top, and rare-top-like samples when present
background_policy.fake_nonprompt_method: not reproducible from open data; save control-proxy counts only
background_policy.electron_charge_misid_method: not reproducible from open data; save opposite-charge application-proxy diagnostics only
background_policy.central_background_uncertainty: flat conservative diagnostic uncertainty plus MC statistical uncertainty unless a richer nuisance model is provided

event_policy.nominal_lepton_requirement: at least two selected leptons before same-sign/trilepton categorization

open_data_execution_contract.branch_contract_summary: All 389 open-data files have the core reco branches needed for the proxy analysis; official auxiliary branches for fake, charge-flip, exact b-tag WP, truth prompt/fake labels, and full nuisance modeling are absent.

open_data_particle_definitions.leptons: Use the ntuple-provided signal-lepton mask `sig_lep` when present; require pT > 28 GeV, |eta| < 2.5, nonzero charge, and type 11 or 13. The open-data branch mask is treated as the authoritative tight/isolation/impact-parameter signal-lepton definition.
open_data_particle_definitions.electrons: Electron candidates are entries with abs(lep_type)==11 passing the signal-lepton mask. The official barrel/endcap crack veto is not separately resolvable beyond the provided `sig_lep` mask unless an explicit electron author/crack-veto branch is added.
open_data_particle_definitions.muons: Muon candidates are entries with abs(lep_type)==13 passing the signal-lepton mask.
open_data_particle_definitions.jets: Use jet_pt, jet_eta, jet_phi, jet_e with pT > 25 GeV and |eta| < 2.5. Apply lepton overlap removal with DeltaR(jet, selected lepton) > 0.4 in the executable workflow.
open_data_particle_definitions.b_tagged_jets: Use `jet_btag_quantile >= 4` as the open-data b-tag definition. Do not call this an exact official 77% or 70% working point unless a calibration note mapping the quantile to that working point is added.
open_data_particle_definitions.missing_transverse_momentum: Use `met` and `met_phi`; `met_mpx` and `met_mpy` are available for cross-checks.
open_data_particle_definitions.trigger: Available trigger branches are `trigE`, `trigM`, `trigMET`, `trigML`, and `TriggerMatch_DILEPTON`. In sampled files `trigML` is present but can be inactive; trigger use must be reported as an open-data approximation.
